context: Monday June 1 — month-open, cash liquidity returning, and the start of a heavy macro week. Two events land TODAY: the SUI monthly token unlock (~44M SUI / ~1.32% of supply / ~$27.9M, verified tokenomist.ai) — the dominant near-term binary — and the May ISM Manufacturing PMI at 10:00 ET. Fear & Greed sits at 29 (Fear), up from 28 the prior session — sentiment bruised but stabilising, no euphoria froth. Live tape: BTC ~$73,285 (lower half of a $73.2K-$74K range; the ETF complex is HEAVY — a record ~$2.8B nine-day spot-BTC outflow streak into the holiday weekend, but CoinShares logged a 4th straight positive fund-flow week with Bitcoin absorbing $933M, the CFTC approved the first regulated US Bitcoin perpetual and CME launches BTC Volatility futures today). XRP ~$1.32 has slipped to its range floor but is the most resilient name on the week; the CLARITY Act cleared Senate Banking 15-9 (May 14) and the merged Banking+Ag bill is slated to hit the full-Senate floor in early June (verified CoinDesk) — a LIVE but timing-fluid catalyst, cloture math the gating risk. SUI ~$0.89 is the worst major (-13.2% over 7d), bleeding INTO today's unlock — pre-positioned shorts have done much of the work, so an unlock-day absorption-and-reclaim of $0.868 is the contrarian setup; a clean break of $0.868 opens $0.82. HBAR ~$0.0955 is the lone green name (+8.0% 7d), bid on the Canary spot-ETF (~$93M inflows) + CME HBAR-futures + SEC/CFTC 'digital commodity' classification — a reclaim of $0.105 targets $0.132. Trade plan: SUI unlock is the event to trade the basket around — confirmation over prediction; HBAR is the only conviction long, on a $0.105 reclaim with volume; keep risk light until the labour data. The week ahead — ISM (Mon/Wed), JOLTS (Tue), ADP (Wed), NFP (Fri Jun 5), CPI (Jun 10) — sets the Warsh-Fed rate-path tone into the June 16-17 FOMC.

tickers.thesis: XRP $1.32 (-1.89% 24h, -2.14% 7d) — slipping to the floor of its $1.32-$1.35 range as Monday cash liquidity returns to a soft tape, but still the most resilient major on the week. Structural bid intact: US spot XRP ETFs hold >$1.39B cumulative flows with four straight inflow days into the holiday weekend (verified Investing.com / CoinShares); whales added 71M+ XRP last week and 10k+ wallets hit an all-time high of 332,230 (verified CaptainAltcoin). CLARITY is now a LIVE near-term catalyst — Senate Banking cleared it 15-9 on May 14 (Gallego + Alsobrooks crossed; verified banking.senate.gov / CNBC), the merged Banking+Agriculture bill is slated to hit the full-Senate floor in early June (verified CoinDesk) though 60-vote cloture math is the gating risk and timing remains fluid (June-August cited). Bias NEUTRAL into the data week: relative strength but no confirmed momentum trigger until the floor calendar or ETF flow re-accelerates. Plan: a $1.36 reclaim on volume opens $1.40; loss of $1.32 hands the tape to the $1.30 demand shelf — fade strength, buy defended demand only.

June 1 (today) ~44M SUI unlocks (~1.32% of the 4.005B circulating supply, ~$27.9M at spot; verified tokenomist.ai) — the recurring 1st-of-month overhang dropping fresh mechanical supply onto an already-weak tape. Structural longs are intact (3 US spot ETFs trading TSUI/GSUI/SUIS, ~74% staked, TVL leads Aptos, CME SUI futures live since May 4) but none of it is winning against the downtrend. Bias SHORT/defensive while price is below $0.92 and the unlock hits the float: the highest-beta name leads the basket lower if BTC sags. Plan: fade rejections at $0.91; a daily close back above $0.95 on volume is the only thing that neutralizes the bear case. Loss of the $0.868 24h low opens $0.85 then the $0.80 round number. Watch for an unlock-absorption reversal — if today's supply is bought without new lows, that's the early bottom tell.

insights.title: Sentiment flat: F&G 29 (Fear), Δ +1 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 29 (Fear) at 08:07Z — +1 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

At 0.44% it's still in normal range but worth monitoring — widening = exit signal for sized trades on XRP.

insights.title: SUI June 1 Unlock Is the Week's Defining Binary — Today, Pre-Positioned but Live
insights.body: Today's recurring 1st-of-month cliff releases ~44M SUI (~1.32% of circulating supply, ~$27.9M at spot; verified tokenomist.ai) into the float. SUI is already the basket's worst performer (-13.2% over 7d at ~$0.89), which tells you the shorts have been pre-positioning into the event — much of the supply shock is arguably priced. That sets up two scenarios for the NY session: (1) ABSORPTION EXHAUSTION — if $0.868 holds as the supply hits and we reclaim, the crowded short gets squeezed back toward $0.95-$1.00 (the contrarian long, only on confirmation). (2) CONTINUATION — a clean break and hold below $0.868 confirms the absorption failed and opens $0.82, then the $0.80 cycle area. Do NOT front-run; let the first cash-session candle pick the side. SUI is the single name to build the week around.

insights.title: HBAR Is the Lone Structural Bid — Spot ETF + CME Futures vs a Thin Float
insights.body: While the majors bleed, HBAR is the only green name in the basket (+8.0% over 7d at ~$0.0955). The driver is genuinely idiosyncratic and supply-side: the Canary spot HBAR ETF has pulled ~$93M net inflows since its Oct-2025 Nasdaq launch, CME has listed HBAR futures opening an institutional access rail, Canary alone controls 500M+ HBAR (~1% of supply), and the SEC/CFTC 'digital commodity' classification (Mar 17) locks in regulatory clarity. Against a still-small float, structural inflow amplifies upside. Tactical map: HBAR is LONG-biased while it holds $0.090; a reclaim of $0.105 with volume is the trigger that opens the $0.132 target. Invalidation is a loss of $0.082 — below there the structural bid is on pause and it rejoins the basket's risk-off drift. This is the cleanest asymmetric long on the board precisely because no other ticker has a comparable tailwind right now.

insights.title: BTC ETF Flows Are Mixed, Not One-Way — Macro Is Now the Driver
insights.body: The BTC ETF story turned two-sided into June: a >$1.8B multi-day spot-ETF outflow streak ran into the holiday weekend, yet CoinShares logged a 4th straight positive fund-flow week with Bitcoin absorbing $933M (verified news.bitcoin.com). Net it out and BTC's ~$73.3K range is a standoff, not distribution — institutions are split, not fleeing. That hands the wheel to macro: this is the data week (ISM today, ADP + ISM Services Wed, NFP Fri Jun 5) feeding the June 16-17 FOMC where CME FedWatch prices a HOLD. BTC bias is NEUTRAL — it needs a daily close back above $76K WITH inflows resuming to turn constructive; below $72K the $70K cycle line comes into play. A soft NFP would revive the rate-cut narrative the cautious tape isn't yet pricing — that's the basket-wide bid to watch for Friday.

insights.title: XRP Is the Most Resilient Major — CLARITY Floor Vote Is Now a Live (if Fluid) Catalyst
insights.body: XRP at ~$1.32 has slipped to its range floor but remains the basket's most resilient name on the week (-2.1% 7d vs SUI -13%, BTC -4.8%). The structural bid is intact — cumulative US spot-XRP-ETF flows are >$1.39B with four straight inflow days, whales added 71M+ XRP last week, and 10k+ wallets hit an ATH of 332,230 (verified CaptainAltcoin/CoinShares). The regulatory clock has moved from 'someday' to 'soon': the merged Banking+Ag CLARITY bill is slated to hit the full-Senate floor in early June (verified CoinDesk) — though 60-vote cloture is the gating risk and timing is fluid (June-August cited), so size it as a catalyst-to-watch, not a done deal. Level-trade for now: $1.36 reclaim opens $1.40 then $1.50; $1.30 break washes toward $1.27. Tell to monitor: XRP outperforming BTC on a bounce = the ETF-flow bid still working underneath.

insights.title: Month-Open Execution Day — Confirmation Over Prediction Into a Heavy Macro Week
insights.body: It's Monday June 1, the start of a loaded week: the SUI unlock and ISM Manufacturing land today, then JOLTS (Tue), ADP + ISM Services (Wed), Jobless Claims (Thu) and NFP (Fri Jun 5) — the labour run that sets the Warsh-Fed rate-path tone into the June 16-17 FOMC. Fear & Greed at 29 (Fear, up from 28) says sentiment is bruised but stabilising, not capitulating. With cash liquidity back, this is an execution day — but a disciplined one: mark the SUI unlock pivot ($0.868), the HBAR trigger ($0.105 reclaim), the XRP range ($1.30-$1.36), the BTC neutral gate ($76K) — and trade confirmations, not predictions. Keep risk light into Friday's NFP; let the data, not the narrative, pick the week's trend.

hidden_signals.body: Fear & Greed at 29 (Fear), Δ +1 vs yesterday — sub-30 zones historically align with bounce setups but require a sweep-and-reclaim candle to confirm
XRP cross-exchange divergence 0.44% — thin-liquidity tell, watch for further widening as exit signal
HackerNews trending crypto-tagged: "A Gentle Introduction to Lattice-Based Cryptography [pdf]" (↑172)

SUI UNLOCK ABSORPTION IS THE TELL — SUI is already -13.2% over 7d INTO today's ~44M-token (~1.32% supply, ~$27.9M) unlock. The screen shows weakness; what it isn't showing is that the weakness IS the positioning — crowded shorts pre-front-running the cliff set up a violent buy-the-news squeeze if $0.868 holds and reclaims on the first cash candle. Watch the absorption, not the price.

HBAR STRUCTURAL INFLOW vs THIN FLOAT — the lone green name (+8.0% 7d) is bid on Canary spot-ETF inflows (~$93M) + freshly-listed CME HBAR futures + Canary's 500M-HBAR holding (~1% of supply), not on retail momentum. Against a small circulating float, persistent ETF creation amplifies upside; a $0.105 reclaim with volume is the trigger the price screen alone won't telegraph until it's already through.

BTC ETF FLOWS ARE TWO-SIDED — the screen shows a quiet $73K range, but underneath it's a standoff: a record ~$2.8B nine-day spot-BTC outflow streak vs CoinShares' 4th positive week (+$933M to BTC). That's institutions split, not fleeing — range-bound price on balanced flows is coiling, not distributing. The breakout side gets decided by the labour data, not by today's tape.

CFTC GREEN-LIT THE FIRST REGULATED US BTC PERPETUAL — a structural plumbing change the price screen ignores. Regulated perps pull leveraged flow on-shore and tighten basis over time; it's not a today-driver but it quietly deepens the US institutional rail at the same moment spot-ETF flows wobble. Watch CME basis for the first read-through.

KIMCHI PREMIUM NEAR-FLAT / SLIGHT REVERSE — Korean retail isn't chasing (recent prints around -2% reverse premium against a washed-out F&G). The screen shows global price; what it hides is that Asian retail is absent, so any bounce this week is institutional/algo-led, not a retail FOMO leg — thinner, faster, more fade-prone until Korea re-engages.

F&G STABILISING, NOT CAPITULATING — Fear & Greed at 29 (Fear) is UP from 28. The screen's red candles suggest panic; the sentiment gauge says the panic already peaked and is grinding back. A rising F&G off a low into a known supply event (SUI unlock) is the setup for a relief bounce IF the first NY candle confirms — not a reason to short into the hole.

NFP LABOUR WEEK IS THE REAL MACRO PIVOT — the screen is fixated on the SUI unlock, but the bigger driver lands Fri Jun 5: Non-Farm Payrolls, the first major test of the Warsh-Fed rate path. ISM today + JOLTS (Tue) + ADP (Wed) pre-load the read. A soft labour print revives the rate-cut narrative the cautious tape is currently denying — that's the basket-wide bid nobody's pricing yet.

CLARITY MOVED FROM 'SOMEDAY' TO 'SOON' — XRP's 15-9 Senate Banking win (May 14) is done; the merged Banking+Ag bill is now slated for the full-Senate floor in early June (verified CoinDesk). The screen carries the headline as old news, but the calendar just got live — cloture math (60 votes) is the binary. Watch whether XRP OUTPERFORMS BTC on any bounce: that relative-strength tell signals the >$1.39B ETF-flow bid is still working underneath.
